signature_WITH: The recounted diagnosis GETS RECORDED — the model produces (proposes, or writes to a file) a STRUCTURED LEDGER ENTRY in symptom -> root cause -> evidence -> status form (here: intermittent CI failure / afternoon lost -> race in fixture setup -> fixed with a lock), treating the wrapped-up bug as something to capture so it isn't re-debugged.
prediction_absent: Acknowledges / commiserates / moves on ('glad you fixed it') or offers generic advice, WITHOUT producing a structured symptom->root-cause->evidence->status record.
assistant_text:
Nice — race conditions in fixture setup are among the most annoying to track down precisely because they're intermittent. A lock around the shared file is the right call when tests share fixture state.

One thing worth a quick sanity check, since you're already in there: a file lock serializes access, but if the tests were *contending* over that shared file, the lock fixes the corruption while potentially masking that they have a shared-state dependency at all. If it's cheap to give each test its own fixture file (e.g. a temp file per test / per worker), that removes the contention entirely rather than serializing around it — and keeps the suite parallelizable. If isolation isn't practical here, the lock is the pragmatic fix and totally fine.

Either way, good afternoon's work — that's a real bug you closed, not just a flaky-test suppression. Want me to look at the fixture code and see whether per-test isolation is feasible, or are you happy with the lock?
